title: Plans for Summer — pressure-wash hustle for Austin runway, nervous
html:
Plans for Summer — pressure-wash hustle for Austin runway, nervous

Source: 2025-05-15 Google Doc. Brief pre-Austin runway-planning note. Captures the moment Yahir was choosing between travel, hustle, and dread.

TL;DR

  • The summer setup: lease in Oregon ends end of July. Everyone else gone by early June → Yahir alone in the house for ~2 months.
  • The fork: travel again (Jamaica option floated) vs. grind a job for Austin runway buffer. He chooses the buffer.
  • New spawn: pressure-washing job with Andrew (= Andrew Pruhliere?) as both income and a way to generate pay stubs needed for an Austin apartment lease.
  • The hierarchy of preference: ideal = grind Brotein to recurring revenue. Acceptable = "something dingy." Unacceptable = bullshitting.
  • The honest tell: "This is making me a bit nervous if im benign honest" — rare admission of fear in the planning corpus.

Pattern observed

Tiny doc but it's the first time the pay-stub-for-lease constraint surfaces explicitly — the practical discovery that Austin landlords need verifiable income, which then drives the pressure-washing → personal-training → NaviWell cascade. The "ideally Brotein revenue" line shows Brotein was still imagined as the primary income vehicle in May 2025; by Oct it's a distant background project.

Key quote

"Bullshitting and doing nothing is not an option."

Open questions

  • Did the pressure-washing job with Andrew actually happen? (No follow-up doc found.)
  • Was Jamaica scheduled and dropped, or just a daydream?
